idea.title: Volatile Sort
idea.hook: Pour colored fluids between tubes like classic ball-sort — except certain color pairs are VOLATILE and detonate if they ever sit adjacent, so you sort to SEPARATE, not just to match.
idea.prototype: Tube/ball-sort puzzle. Tap a source tube then a destination tube to pour the top run of same-colored balls (standard ball-sort rules); same colors stack. A volatility table marks color pairs (e.g. red+yellow) that explode and fail the level if they ever become vertically adjacent in any tube. WIN when every tube is single-color (or empty) with no volatile reaction ever triggered. LOSE when a banned pair touches OR the pour budget runs out. Volatile pairs are telegraphed by a pulsing warning glyph between the two color swatches in a HUD danger banner plus glowing halos on at-risk tubes.
idea.why_novel: sort to SEPARATE volatile pairs, not just match — every ball-sort and our own Hue Sort reward grouping like-with-like; Volatile Sort overlays an adjacency-hazard constraint that makes some merges lethal, so the optimal path must route AROUND reactions, an avoidance layer the sort genre on these portals never adds.
idea.lose_condition: A volatile color pair becomes vertically adjacent in any tube (volatileHit), OR pours run out before all tubes are sorted (pours <= 0 while unsolved).
idea.hook_ru: Переливай цветные жидкости между пробирками как в классической сортировке шаров — но некоторые пары цветов ВЗРЫВООПАСНЫ и детонируют, оказавшись рядом: сортируй, чтобы РАЗДЕЛИТЬ их, а не просто собрать по цвету.

sources.name: Ball Sort Puzzle - Color Sort
sources.note: base genre being mutated (Yandex top sorting cluster)

sources.name: Block Dropping Merge
sources.note: CTR reference — object scale / clear color groups

synthesizer_brief: Daily MARKET ANCHOR (signal) slot — mechanic-led. Take the proven ball-sort / tube-sort genre (huge install base on Yandex + topGrossing presence via Royal Match on Google Play, plus fresh CrazyGames momentum on ball games like Ball Battle Simulator) and add ONE twist that inverts the goal: certain color pairs are VOLATILE and detonate if they ever sit adjacent in a tube. The player no longer sorts purely to MATCH — they sort to SEPARATE, routing dangerous colors apart. Pressure is a finite pour budget (moves budget, NOT a timer). Critical readability requirement from portfolio data ('unclear puzzles get downvoted'): keep the base ball-sort intuitively obvious and telegraph the volatile twist hard — a pulsing warning glyph + color swatches in a HUD danger banner, plus glowing halos on at-risk tubes, so a new player understands the goal in the first 5 seconds. Mobile-portrait-first; every tube is a large reachable tap target. Cover borrows object scale / contrast / clear color groups from the puzzle_objects CTR references (Block Dropping Merge, Merge Dale, Monster Match, Vega Mix 2) but stays honest to the tube-sort mechanic.
